title: Race-lap peer-relative normalization features
summary: F1 strategy depends on relative rather than absolute pace: out-lap strength, warm-up, field degradation, and track evolution matter.
rationale: F1 strategy depends on relative rather than absolute pace: out-lap strength, warm-up, field degradation, and track evolution matter. The current best code uses absolute values plus global frequencies, but not where a driver sits relative to the field on that exact lap/race context.
implementation_hint: Within each (Year,Race,LapNumber) and optionally (Year,Race,LapNumber,Compound), compute percentile/rank/z-score style features for TyreLife, LapTime_Delta, Cumulative_Degradation, Position_Change, and current stint age. Add gaps to race-lap median, fastest quartile, and same-compound median. Also test interaction features like relative_tyre_age x position and relative_lap_delta x compound.
expected_effect: Should capture hidden pit waves and track-evolution effects without requiring explicit rival identities or future information.
risk: If computed jointly on train+test or across folds, these features leak race-level distribution information; must be generated inside each fold and separately on inference races.
